item.title: Trump says "I love the inflation" because U.S. is "taking out" Iranian oil
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. has taken out "millions" of barrels of Iranian oil in the dead of night, and said inflation will come down when the war ends.

item.title: American's birth rate has plunged. Are smartphones to blame?
item.description: The iPhone was introduced in 2007, the same year the U.S. birth rate started to slide. The issues could be linked, a new analysis finds.

item.title: Trump vows to hit Iran "hard," Hegseth says U.S. will "negotiate with bombs"
item.description: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire is appearing to crumble as President Trump pledges new strikes on Iran tonight, warning Tehran will "pay the price" for not accepting a deal.

item.title: How to buy SpaceX shares as its blockbuster IPO readies for liftoff
item.description: SpaceX is setting aside a large chunk of shares for ordinary investors as it seeks to raise a record $75 billion. Here's what to know.

item.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
item.description: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

item.title: Bill Gates tells Congress that meeting Epstein was "a grave error in judgment"
item.description: Bill Gates told members of Congress on Wednesday that Jeffrey Epstein put his philanthropic work at risk, and that meeting him represented "a grave error in judgment."

item.title: Vance says Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong"
item.description: The vice president said President Trump "has been very clear about what is in our best interest" as the relationship with Israel is tested over the Iran war.

item.title: Will SpaceX's blockbuster IPO soar or sputter? Past IPOs offer clues.
item.description: Splashy initial public offerings often skyrocket early on, only to return to earth with a thud, Wall Street analysts say.

item.title: Burning cross in Chicago's Grant Park under investigation
item.description: A cross was set on fire Tuesday afternoon in Chicago's Grant Park, shocking people who saw it. Police are investigating, but no arrests have been made. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: NASA chief defends choice of all-male crew for Artemis III mission
item.description: NASA's Jared Isaacman says the crew was selected solely based on their experience, expertise and availability for flight assignment.

item.title: CD account moves savers should make with inflation rising again
item.description: Inflation just rose to its highest level since 2023. Here are three CD account moves savers should make in response.

item.title: When will the Iran war end? Here's what Trump has said.
item.description: President Trump has offered timelines of days and weeks for the Iran war, but a solution remains elusive.

item.title: What caused inflation to soar to its highest point in 3 years?
item.description: Inflation surged again in May due to high energy costs and supply constraints worsened by the Iran war. Madison Mills, a senior reporter at Axios, joins to discuss.

item.title: Vance says Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong"
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong," but has ultimately "been a good partner" to the U.S. "We're gonna keep on working together. But where interests diverge, the United States is gonna pursue the best interests of our nation, and that's how it's gonna be," Vance told Robert Costa in an interview airing Sunday.

item.title: World's largest whale graveyard found by sub at bottom of ocean
item.description: The researchers saw many strange animals — many believed to be new to science — living off the whale carcasses.

item.title: Iran, U.S. trade strikes overnight as war appears to reignite
item.description: U.S. officials say they hit nearly 20 Iranian targets overnight in response to the downing of an Apache helicopter earlier this week. CBS News' Eleanor Watson and Natalie Brand report.

item.title: ICE mandates additional training for new hires after backlash
item.description: ICE has extended training for new officers and mandated additional instruction for those onboarded under a shortened process that has now been scrapped.

item.title: Who qualifies for Social Security disability insurance?
item.description: Social Security disability benefits can provide crucial income support, but eligibility is notoriously strict.

item.title: Passersby left shocked after burning cross spotted in Grant Park
item.description: The flames from the cross burning in the middle of Grant Park were visible to anyone driving or walking down Columbus Drive.

item.title: Details emerge from Bill Gates' testimony in Epstein probe
item.description: Bill Gates is testifying before the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday about his ties to Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News' Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: Just 1 in 10 Europeans now view the U.S. as an ally, according to poll
item.description: A growing number of Europeans see the U.S. as a rival or an adversary, especially in Denmark, France, Spain and Switzerland, according to the poll.

item.title: U.S. Embassy in Mexico warns Americans of safety risks ahead of World Cup
item.description: The U.S. Embassy said Americans traveling to Mexico for the World Cup should look at advisories for the region they will be visiting.

item.title: Trump vows U.S. will strike Iran again today: "They keep playing us for suckers"
item.description: President Trump said Iran will "pay the price" for taking too long to negotiate a deal, adding that the U.S. will hit Iran "hard." This comes after a U.S. Apache helicopter was downed, sparking an exchange of fire. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes has the latest.

item.title: Violent anti-immigration protests erupt in Belfast after stabbing attack
item.description: Despite calls for calm, masked anti-immigration protesters in Belfast set a bus, vehicles and some homes on fire in what one politician called "a race-based pogrom."

item.title: Alabama nitrogen gas executions are cruel and unusual punishment, judge says
item.description: A federal judge banned Alabama from executing an inmate by nitrogen hypoxia, calling the method unconstitutionally cruel.

item.title: Honda recalls 880,000 vehicles over possible rear suspension issue
item.description: The recall affects certain Honda Pilot, Ridgeline, Passport, and Acura MDX vehicles sold in 23 states and the District of Columbia.

item.title: Oversight chair working to have Blanche testify in Epstein probe
item.description: House Oversight and Reform Committee Chairman James Comer said he wants Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to appear before lawmakers in July.

item.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
item.description: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."

item.title: Pilot allegedly flew 900 flights without valid license: "Like a movie script"
item.description: "This is similar to a doctor that is licensed to practice family medicine but is doing brain surgery in their office," police said.

item.title: What's the mortgage interest rate forecast for summer 2026? Here's what some experts predict.
item.description: Before you start comparing your mortgage loan options, make sure you know where interest rates could head next.
